title: Add --enable-webhooks CLI flag to gate webhook server and registration
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#1](https://github.com/C5C3/memcached-operator/issues/1)

Add an `--enable-webhooks` boolean CLI flag (default `true`) to `cmd/main.go` that conditionally creates the webhook server and registers admission webhooks. When `false`, skip `webhook.NewServer()` creation (lines 104-106), pass nil `WebhookServer` to `ctrl.NewManager`, skip `SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager()` call (lines 141-144), and log an info message. Update `charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` (lines 36-45) to pass `--enable-webhooks=false` when `.Values.webhook.enabled` is false. Add table-driven unit test in `cmd/main_test.go` following the existing `TestParseWatchNamespaces` pattern (lines 10-98).

**Rationale:** The operator pod enters CrashLoopBackOff when deployed with `webhook.enabled: false` because the binary unconditionally starts the webhook server and tries to load TLS certs from a volume that isn't mounted. This blocks webhook-less deployments needed for kind clusters and CI environments where cert-manager TLS provisioning is problematic. Observed in production CI with `context deadline exceeded` on Helm install.

**Affected Areas:**
- cmd/main.go
- cmd/main_test.go
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical

## Summary

The operator binary unconditionally creates a webhook server and registers admission webhooks in `cmd/main.go:104-106` and `cmd/main.go:141-144`, regardless of whether TLS certificates are mounted. When deployed with `webhook.enabled: false` (no cert volume), the webhook server fails to load TLS certs, the health probe fails, and the pod enters CrashLoopBackOff. The fix adds an `--enable-webhooks` CLI flag (default `true`) that gates webhook server creation and webhook registration, with both the Helm chart and kustomize base updated to support disabling webhooks.

## Scope

**Included:**
- New `--enable-webhooks` boolean flag in `cmd/main.go` (default: `true`, added alongside existing flags at lines 63-76)
- Conditional webhook server creation gated on the flag (`cmd/main.go:104-106` — `webhook.NewServer()` only called when flag is true)
- Conditional `SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager` call gated on the flag (`cmd/main.go:141-144`)
- Info-level log message when webhooks are disabled (operator observability)
- Helm `charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` passes `--enable-webhooks=false` in the args block (lines 36-45) when `.Values.webhook.enabled` is false
- Kustomize `config/manager/manager.yaml` base manifest (lines 38-42) — no change needed in the base since the flag defaults to `true`; the existing `config/default/kustomization.yaml` webhook overlay already adds webhook resources, so deploying without the webhook overlay naturally means no certs/ports, and the flag can be added via a kustomize patch for webhook-disabled deployments
- Unit test in `cmd/main_test.go` following the existing table-driven pattern (see `TestParseWatchNamespaces` at lines 10-98)

**Excluded:**
- Changes to webhook logic (`api/v1beta1/memcached_webhook.go`, `memcached_validation_webhook.go`) — not affected, YAGNI
- Changes to controller/reconciler — not affected by this flag
- Helm `values.yaml` changes — `webhook.enabled` already exists and correctly gates all other chart resources (ports, volumes, cert-manager CRs)
- Integration test suite changes (`internal/controller/suite_test.go`) — envtest always runs with webhooks enabled, not affected
- E2E / Chainsaw test for webhooks-disabled mode — separate concern
- Graceful degradation / runtime toggle — YAGNI, a static flag at startup is sufficient

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start["main.go startup"] --> ParseFlags["Parse CLI flags"]
    ParseFlags --> CheckFlag{"--enable-webhooks?"}

    CheckFlag -->|true| CreateWH["Create webhook.Server with TLS opts"]
    CreateWH --> CreateMgr["ctrl.NewManager with WebhookServer"]
    CreateMgr --> RegCtrl["Register MemcachedReconciler"]
    RegCtrl --> RegWH["SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager"]
    RegWH --> Probes["Register health/readiness probes"]
    Probes --> Run["mgr.Start"]

    CheckFlag -->|false| LogSkip["Log: webhooks disabled"]
    LogSkip --> SkipWH["WebhookServer = nil"]
    SkipWH --> CreateMgrNoWH["ctrl.NewManager without WebhookServer"]
    CreateMgrNoWH --> RegCtrlOnly["Register MemcachedReconciler"]
    RegCtrlOnly --> SkipRegWH["Skip webhook registration"]
    SkipRegWH --> Probes
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Helm["Helm Chart"]
        Values["values.yaml\nwebhook.enabled: false"]
        Deploy["deployment.yaml"]
        Values -->|"if not .Values.webhook.enabled"| Deploy
    end
    Deploy -->|"--enable-webhooks=false"| Binary["Operator Binary"]
    Binary -->|"flag=false"| NoWH["Skip webhook server + registration"]
```

## Key Components

- **`cmd/main.go` — flag definition + conditional logic**: Add `enableWebhooks` bool flag at line ~68 (alongside existing flag vars). Gate `webhook.NewServer()` at lines 104-106 and `SetupMemcachedWebhookWithManager()` at lines 141-144 on the flag. Add info log when webhooks are skipped.
- **`charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` — Helm arg injection** (lines 36-45): Add `--enable-webhooks=false` to the args block when `.Values.webhook.enabled` is false, following the existing conditional arg pattern (`{{- if not .Values.webhook.enabled }}`).
- **`config/manager/manager.yaml` — kustomize base manifest** (lines 38-42): No change needed in the base (flag defaults to `true`). For webhook-disabled kustomize deployments, users omit the `config/webhook/` and `config/certmanager/` resources from their kustomization and add a patch with `--enable-webhooks=false`. The existing overlay structure in `config/default/kustomization.yaml` already separates webhook resources.
- **`cmd/main_test.go` — unit test**: Add a test verifying the flag is parsed correctly and that webhook server creation is conditional, following the existing table-driven pattern used by `TestParseWatchNamespaces`.
